user
The canvas has five rectangles of the same size scattered around at
different heights and uneven spacing.

Arrange them into one neat horizontal row so that:

  1. All five boxes are aligned along the same top edge (their tops line up
     exactly).
  2. The gaps between neighbouring boxes are all equal (the boxes are evenly
     distributed left-to-right).

The boxes must not overlap, and you must not resize them — keep each box the
size it already is. Their left-to-right order does not matter.
Getting the tops to line up and the gaps to come out equal by dragging each
box by hand is very hard to do precisely. Select all five boxes at once
(drag a selection box around them, or shift-click each) — when more than one
object is selected, Excalidraw shows alignment and distribution controls you
can use to align their tops and space them evenly in one step.

The boxes are all still stacked on top of each other. It looks like the "Distribute Horizontally" button didn't spread them out. This is because all the boxes are at the same x position and distribution won't work when all boxes have the same x - the distribution needs them to span a range.

The problem is: when I clicked "Align Top" (button 1 in row 2), it also aligned them all to the same LEFT position (they were already left-aligned from before). So all 5 boxes are now exactly stacked at the same point.

I need a different strategy. Let me:
1. Undo both the "Align Top" and the "Distribute Horizontal" operations
2. Go back to the original positions
3. Then apply Align Top first, then Distribute Horizontally

The key insight is: the "Distribute Horizontally" only works when boxes have different X positions. Since they all got aligned to the left AND the top, they're all on top of each other.

The "Align Top" aligns tops but keeps X positions. Since they're all at the same X, it stacks them all.
The "Distribute Horizontally" distributes based on their X positions. Since they were already at the same X, it can't distribute them.

I need to use a **different order or approach**:
1. First, I need to go back to the **original scattered positions** (where boxes have different X positions)
2. Then apply "Align Top" to align their Y positions
3. Then apply "Distribute Horizontally" to space them evenly

The problem with the current state (all boxes at same X, different Y): 
- "Align Top" will align all to same Y → they pile up
- "Distribute Horizontally" won't help when all have same X

**New strategy**: Since the boxes are currently stacked vertically, I need to first:
1. **Distribute Horizontally** first (to spread them out on the X-axis while keeping their Y positions different)
2. Then **Align Top** (to align their Y positions)

I can see all Align buttons. Now, the current state is: All boxes are stacked vertically (same X, different Y). 

My new plan:

2. Then click **Align Top** (button 1 in row 2, at position 44, 656) to align their tops

Wait - but if all boxes have the SAME X coordinate (left-aligned), then "Distribute Horizontally" would distribute them starting from the leftmost to the rightmost, but since they're all at the same point, the leftmost = rightmost and they'd stay stacked.

I think the real issue here is that I need to go back to having them at different X positions (the original scattered layout). Let me think about what undo state I need...

Actually, looking at this more carefully: The "Distribute Horizontally" operation in Excalidraw distributes objects to have equal **gaps** between them. It takes the leftmost and rightmost positions as boundaries and distributes the others in between. Since all objects are at the same X here, it won't help.
